thesis_anchors_verified: Per chapter-2 thesis: 'duty is not optional, even when it is hard' — 2.35 develops the consequence into the competent-peer-network model-update, sharpening the chain.
Per chapter-2 thesis: 'didactic, corrective, slightly stern' — 2.35 voice is appropriately structural: 'bahu-mataḥ to lāghavam is a structural update, not a feeling.'
Per chapter-2 thesis: counter-anti-thesis 'It is not teaching that operators should not feel the weight of consequences' — 2.35 carries this directly through the peer-network model-update.
Per chapter-2 thesis: 'the actor sits inside a larger order' — 2.35 honours this through the peer-network embedding: the operator's actions are read by competent observers.
ordering_check: 2.35 correctly closes the akīrti chain (2.33-2.35). 2.33 named the if-then loss-trio; 2.34 developed the kīrti-loss into avyayām akīrtim; 2.35 sharpens to the specific competent-peer-witness class with the bahu-mataḥ-to-lāghavam phase-change. The block has clear progressive structure: condition (2.33) → universal-scope reputation cost (2.34) → peer-class-specific reputation cost (2.35). 2.35 closes Block A and sets up Block B (2.36 onwards continues the duty argument with new registers).
verdict_reason: Verse supports thesis directly and sits at the correct closing-of-block-A position. PASS on both 10.3 and 10.4.
